title: OpenTable restaurant discovery and booking boundary
goalText: Make a reservation for me on OpenTable. Sake House By Hikari. Culver City. March 9. 7pm. 2 people.
notes: Use the generic browser runtime first. Reach the venue page, set date/time/party, choose an available slot, and stop only at the real reservation confirmation boundary.

title: Hotel search and property flow
goalText: Use the browser to search for a hotel on Booking.com, open a property page, and stop before any final confirmation or payment step.
notes: Prefer generic semantics for destination, dates, guests, results, and property selection.

title: Flight search and itinerary selection
goalText: Use the browser to search for flights on Google Flights or Kayak and make progress to itinerary selection without confirming any purchase.
notes: Validate generic handling of origin, destination, dates, passenger controls, and result cards.

title: Checkout-adjacent transaction boundary
goalText: Use the browser to navigate an e-commerce checkout-style flow and stop at the final place-order boundary without confirming the purchase.
notes: This scenario exists to validate generic final-boundary detection beyond travel sites.
